items.title: Kernels
items.description: Linear SVM in this topic maximizes margin in input space. The kernel trick replaces xᵢᵀx with K(xᵢ, x) to learn nonlinear boundaries without explicit high-dimensional features — RBF, polynomial, and dual-form details live in Kernels.

items.title: Logistic Regression
items.description: Both learn linear decision boundaries, but logistic regression minimizes BCE and outputs P(y=1|x) via sigmoid; SVM maximizes margin with hinge slack and outputs sign(wᵀx + b). Pick logistic regression for calibrated probabilities; SVM for margin-based separation and sparse support vectors.

items.title: Norms & Distances
items.description: Geometric margin uses the L2 norm ||w||₂ and point-to-hyperplane distance. Norms & Distances defines L1/L2/L∞ and Euclidean distance — SVM applies L2 geometry to measure margin width in feature space.

items.title: ROC & AUC
items.description: SVM decision_function scores rank examples for ROC curves and AUC without default calibrated probabilities. ROC & AUC covers TPR/FPR trade-offs; here we produce the f(x) scores those metrics consume.

items.title: k-Nearest Neighbors
items.description: k-NN stores all training points and votes locally; SVM retains only support vectors and a global max-margin hyperplane. Both can model nonlinear boundaries (kernels vs. local neighborhoods) with opposite memory and inference costs.
